chief_complaint: A 40-year-old woman presented with a 7-year history of recurrent choking episodes and chronic occipital headaches.
history: The patient had a 7-year history of recurrent choking episodes affecting solids and liquids, nocturnal choking episodes occurring 3-4 times weekly, and chronic occipital headaches spanning 14 years. Home monitoring during the COVID-19 pandemic incidentally revealed episodes of severe oxygen desaturation (70%-80%) without respiratory distress. Multiple laryngoscopies and endoscopies performed to investigate the choking episodes were normal.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly stated in the case report.
labs: MRI of the head and neck confirmed Chiari malformation type I (CMI) with 11 mm cerebellar tonsillar descent. Sleep study demonstrated mild sleep apnoea (apnoea-hypopnea index 6.0) with predominantly central events (72%).
